{{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado]] section of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
[[ja:多度大社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado]] section of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{ja icon}}
[[Category:Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]]
[[ja:多度大社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado]] section of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on [[May 4]] and [[May 5|5]] every year.
==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{ja icon}}
[[Category:Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]]
[[ja:多度大社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado]] section of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on [[May 4]] and [[May 5|5]] every year.
==Festivals== *[[Tado Festival]]: The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. ([[May 4]]–[[May 5|5]]) *Chōchin Festival: A lantern festival. (Saturday and Sunday in late-July) *Yabusame Festival: A horseback riding archery competition. ([[November 23]])
==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{ja icon}}
[[Category:Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]]
[[ja:多度大社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado]] section of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on [[May 4]] and [[May 5|5]] every year. The shrine has five nationally-designated Important Cultural Properties and one prefecturally-designated.
==Festivals== *[[Tado Festival]]: The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. ([[May 4]]–[[May 5|5]]) *Chōchin Festival: A lantern festival. (Saturday and Sunday in late-July) *Yabusame Festival: A horseback riding archery competition. ([[November 23]])
==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{ja icon}}

It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally-designated and one prefecturally-designated [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]] . It was formerly a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{ja icon}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} {{coord|35|08|07.8|N|136|37|21.5|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]]

[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]] 511-0106 | phone = | website = http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ }} [[Image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]] area of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]]. It was formerly a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{ja icon}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]]

[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]] 511-0106 | website = {{URL|http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[Image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]] area of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]]. It was formerly a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]]

[[Mie Prefecture|Mie]] 511-0106 | website = {{URL|http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]] area of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]]. It was formerly a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]]. [[Mie Prefecture|Mie]] 511-0106 | website = {{URL|http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]] area of the city of [[Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[Mie Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan|Important Cultural Properties]]. It was formerly a national shrine of the first rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tado Taisha]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tado Taisha]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tado Taisha]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tado Taisha]] [[en:Tado Taisha]] [[Category:New]]

多度大社 | image = Tado Taisha01.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|08|07.8|N|136|37|21.5|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Amatsuhikone]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1681 Tado, [[:en:Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]], [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]]
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie]] 511-0106 | website = {{URL|http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]] area of the city of [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is well known for its [[Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. It was formerly a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[:en:Chōchin|Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] [[sn:Tado Taisha]] [[en:Tado Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{Commons category|Tado Shrine}} [[fr:Tado-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Tado]] [[ja:多度大社]] [[zh:多度大社]]

多度大社 | image = Tado Taisha01.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|08|07.8|N|136|37|21.5|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Amatsuhikone]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1681 Tado, [[:en:Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]], [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]]
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie]] 511-0106 | website = {{URL|http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Tado, Mie|Tado-chō]] area of the city of [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana]] in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is well known for its [[:en:Tado Festival|Tado Festival]], which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. It was formerly a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''[[:en:Tado Festival|Tado Festival]]''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[:en:Chōchin|Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] [[en:Tado Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tado Shrine}} [[fr:Tado-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Tado]] [[ja:多度大社]] [[zh:多度大社]] {{wikidata link|Q116140}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tado Shrine]][[en:Tado Taisha]][[fr:Tado-taisha]][[it:Santuario Tado]][[ja:多度大社]][[zh:多度大社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tado Shrine]][[en:Tado Taisha]][[fr:Tado-taisha]][[it:Santuario Tado]][[ja:多度大社]][[zh:多度大社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tado Shrine]][[en:Tado Taisha]][[fr:Tado-taisha]][[it:Santuario Tado]][[ja:多度大社]][[zh:多度大社]] {{infobox religious building | name = Tado Taisha
多度大社 | image = Tado Taisha01.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|08|07.8|N|136|37|21.5|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Amatsuhikone]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1681 Tado, {{ill|Tado, Mie|en|Tado, Mie|WD=Q7674654|lt=Tado-chō|1=Tado, Mie}}, {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana|1=Kuwana, Mie}}
{{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie|1=Mie Prefecture}} 511-0106 | website = {{URL|http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tado Shrine'''|多度大社|Tado Taisha}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Tado, Mie|en|Tado, Mie|WD=Q7674654|lt=Tado-chō|1=Tado, Mie}} area of the city of {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana|1=Kuwana, Mie}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is well known for its {{ill|Tado Festival|en|Tado Festival|WD=Q116021|lt=Tado Festival|1=Tado Festival}}, which takes place on May 4 and 5 every year. The shrine has five nationally designated and one prefecturally designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. It was formerly a national shrine of the first Rank (国幣大社, ''kokuhei taisha'') in the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. ==Festivals== * '''{{ill|Tado Festival|en|Tado Festival|WD=Q116021|lt=Tado Festival|1=Tado Festival}}''' (May 4–5): The largest of the events at the shrine, it involves young men riding horses up a hill and over a wall. * '''[[:en:Chōchin|Chōchin]] Festival''' (Saturday and Sunday in late-July): A lantern festival. * '''[[Yabusame]] Festival''' (November 23): A horseback riding archery competition. ==External links== *[http://www.tadotaisya.or.jp/ Tado Shrine homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Commonscat|Tado Shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[image:Tado Shrine.jpg|thumb|right|The entrance to Tado Shrine during the Tado Festival]] [[en:Tado Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tado Shrine}} [[fr:Tado-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Tado]] [[ja:多度大社]] [[zh:多度大社]] {{wikidata link|Q116140}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Senior Second Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Second Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
